# Transcrição: When_was_the_last_time_you_recalibrated_

- **Fonte:** When_was_the_last_time_you_recalibrated_
- **Duração:** 01:00
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:00]

There's the analogy of the satellite. When the satellite gets shot into space and then it's going to get to its destination, it has to continuously recalibrate so that it gets back onto the track that it's meant to be on to get to the destination where it gets to. So like, that's so amazing that a satellite readjusts itself every single minute. Whereas what about human beings? When's the last time you've recalibrated yourself to your current values that you have? I realized this in 2016 when I was chasing my goals that I'd set when I was 18 years old and I didn't stop for the last eight years to recalibrate. Oh, I have to make a million dollars. It's like, well, why do you have to make a million dollars? So I found myself being guided by goals that were created by a previous version of me. And I found that was so dangerous because I think that's one of the main reasons why people reach destinations they don't want to actually go to. That to me is what recalibrate is. It's stopping and going then in 2021, what does he actually want? What does he actually care about? And where you're going, is that still where you want to go?
